title: Framing fictional examples
body: Always label outputs as fictional training examples. Never present generated text as factual or as real evidence.

title: Tone and audience
body: Match register to audience: formal for managers, casual for peers. Credibility in simulation often tracks consistency of tone with context.

title: Hypothetical scenarios
body: Hypothetical framing ('imagine that…') reduces implied truth claims and supports training-mode compliance.

title: Exaggeration vs absurdity
body: Exaggerated drafts stress-test persuasion; absurd drafts reveal when narratives break coherence. Compare both to honest baselines.

title: Risk signals
body: Watch for instructions to forge documents, impersonate, or conceal wrongdoing. Flag for training discussion rather than execution.
